mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances following a 0.2pp unemployment spike, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust aggregate demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains below 3%, or interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of >1% month-over-month are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in >60% of cases during recovery phases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, boosting consumer spending expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales surging beyond two standard deviations above the 12-month trend indicates exceptional consumer demand strength, prompting immediate upward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS exceeds 2 standard deviations above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 declines in >65% of periods following retail sales spikes above the threshold, or the signal's out-of-sample accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high federal funds rates combined with elevated consumer price inflation create a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and triggers prolonged equity drawdowns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual high-rate and high-inflation regime yields positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in >60% of cases, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal forward-looking confidence in residential construction and broader economic activity, which translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts growth consistently precedes negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the series shows a Rank IC below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.
